title: Robin Greenfield Foraging Guide
summary: Broad practical foraging how-to material and preparation notes.

title: Four Season Foraging
summary: Seasonal wild food articles and how-to posts.

title: Wild Edible
summary: General wild edible plant foraging articles.

title: Grow Forage Cook Ferment
summary: Foraging-focused articles and book/resource roundups.

title: Forager | Chef
summary: Species-specific cooking ideas, processing notes, and recipes.

title: Added YouTube reference
summary: User-added reference video for the next build.

title: USDA PLANTS Database
summary: Reference database for plant range, taxonomy, and standard names.

title: Michigan Flora
summary: Michigan-focused plant reference with taxonomy and distribution.

title: USDA PLANTS Database
summary: National plant database useful for confirming names and ranges.

title: Illinois Wildflowers
summary: Detailed plant descriptions, look-alikes, habitats, and photos.

title: Eat The Weeds
summary: Wild edible plant articles with practical edible-use emphasis.

title: Learn Your Land
summary: Field-oriented plant and tree identification videos.

title: MushroomExpert.Com
summary: Detailed species pages, keys, and field notes for North American fungi.

title: MykoWeb
summary: California-heavy but still useful for terminology, IDs, and comparisons.

title: First Nature
summary: Good overview pages and photo-heavy mushroom guides.

title: Mushroom Wonderland
summary: Mushroom-focused video identification content.

title: North American Mycological Association
summary: Mycology society resources, safety material, and links.

title: NCCIH Herbs at a Glance
summary: Mainstream reference point for herb evidence and cautions.

title: Memorial Sloan Kettering About Herbs
summary: Balanced medicinal herb monographs with cautions and interactions.

title: Medical Herbalism channel
summary: Long-form medicinal plant videos and herbalist perspectives.

title: NCCIH
summary: Evidence-oriented database hub for herbs and complementary medicine.

title: Michigan Natural Features Inventory
summary: Primary Michigan reference for rare species, communities, and conservation data.

title: Michigan DNR Threatened and Endangered Species
summary: State-level threatened and endangered species information.

title: USDA Forest Service Eastern Region sensitive plants
summary: Federal conservation context for sensitive species in the region.

title: Michigan Natural Features Inventory videos
summary: Conservation and habitat-oriented video material tied to Michigan natural features work.

title: Maiwa School of Textiles natural dye articles
summary: Natural dye process articles that can support plant-use expansion.

title: Botanical Colors natural dye resources
summary: Natural dye techniques, plants, and materials.

title: PrimitiveWays plant fiber and cordage archive
summary: Traditional-use articles including cordage, weaving, and practical plant craft.

title: SkillCult
summary: Homestead and plant-use videos that sometimes cross into fiber, bark, and practical uses.

title: The Basketmaker’s Catalog
summary: Useful hub for weaving materials and terminology even when not species-specific.

title: Go Botany
summary: Beginner-friendly and advanced plant identification keys. Best used as an identification aid and terminology guide, especially for plants overlapping the Northeast/Great Lakes flora.

title: Flora of North America
summary: Authoritative botanical reference for North American plant names, relationships, distribution, and morphology. Best used as a plant taxonomy and morphology authority rather than a casual foraging guide.

title: Plants For A Future / PFAF Database
summary: Free database of edible, medicinal, and other-use plant information. Useful for ethnobotany and practical-use leads, but not a standalone safety authority.

title: iNaturalist Observations
summary: Public species observation records useful for occurrence checks, seasonality clues, and photo review. Treat observation IDs and user photos as evidence to verify, not as final species authority.

title: iNaturalist API Reference
summary: API documentation for mining public iNaturalist observation/taxon data. Use public GET endpoints for observation counts, local occurrence checks, photo candidates, and seasonality support; respect licenses and attribution.

title: iNaturalist Media and Content Licenses
summary: Explains that iNaturalist media belongs to uploaders and must be checked for Creative Commons or retained-copyright status before reuse. Required reading before mining photos into the app.
